recent_calls.summary: Danisha called after hours to report that her AC is not working and expressed interest in scheduling a service appointment. The agent documented the issue and informed her that a team member will call back during regular business hours to assist with scheduling. Danisha also inquired about emergency services, but the agent explained that emergency dispatch is not available after hours.
The user called to report that their AC system is not cooling. The agent confirmed the user's address and asked how to assist further, but the call ended shortly after without resolution.

The user called to report that the AC in their HVAC system purchased from the company is not cooling. The agent asked for the service address ZIP code to assist further, but the call ended abruptly with the user hanging up after repeating 'Hello'.

Elesia called to request a cooling service appointment for her unit at 3630 Stonehaven Drive, which is not cooling properly. She requested service as soon as possible and provided details about the furnace location and recent service history. The agent confirmed the request, noted her interest in maintenance program information, and assured that the team would follow up soon.

The user, Cory, called back to schedule an air conditioner repair appointment for a capacitor issue, preferring the earliest possible time on Monday. The agent confirmed the service address and contact details, noted the request, and offered information about maintenance programs, which Cory declined. The call ended with the agent thanking Cory and no further requests made.

The user reported an emergency backup in their basement main drain and was informed that after-hours service is unavailable. The agent documented the emergency and assured the user that the team will contact them first thing on the next business day. The user confirmed no further assistance was needed.
The user called back after receiving a message and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted briefly and ended with a successful transfer.

The user reported that their air conditioner is still making a loud vibrating noise after a recent service call. The agent confirmed the user's contact details and service address, assured the user that the message would be passed to the team, and concluded the call after the user had no further requests.

The user called to inquire about a previous quote for a new AC unit and requested either the quote details or a follow-up visit. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another agent. The transfer was completed successfully within a short call duration.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent's initial greeting and request for the user's ZIP code. The user did not respond before hanging up.
The user called and requested to speak to a live agent. The initial agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to another agent as requested.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.

Nicole Barnes called to request a service for her air conditioner not turning on under her American Home Shield home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details and confirmed the service appointment preferences. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to ask whether they should turn off the power to their unit before a technician arrives due to freezing issues. The agent advised leaving the unit as is for safety and then transferred the user to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Deborah Wartswa, called to arrange a repair appointment for her mother's house and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to reschedule a cooling service appointment because the technician did not show up as planned and the issue with the air conditioner persists. The agent confirmed the details and noted the user's preference for a late afternoon appointment, promising that the team will reach out the next business day to confirm. The call ended with the user providing no additional instructions.

The user called to inquire about the timing of a scheduled appointment as they needed to leave by six but were unsure if the technician would arrive on time. The agent explained that since it was after-hours, they could not check the technician's status but assured the user's message would be passed to the team for follow-up on the next business day. The user did not request to reschedule during the call.

The user, Luwanika, called to schedule a repair for her air conditioner, which has been out for over a week due to a needed blower motor replacement. The agent documented the urgent situation and escalated it for follow-up on the next business day, as it was after regular hours. The call ended with the user confirming no further assistance was needed at the moment.

The user called to request an emergency air conditioning recharge for their duplex at 2319 Indiana Ave, St Louis, MO 63104, unit 2F. The agent documented the request and prioritized it for the next business day since it was after hours, informing the user that a technician would contact them then. The call ended with the user declining further assistance.

Michael called to follow up on a previous service regarding a frozen evaporator coil in his air conditioning unit. He requested that the estimate for the coil replacement be resent and scheduled a follow-up for Monday or Saturday. Michael declined additional maintenance program information due to his existing American Home Shield coverage and provided positive feedback about the call.

The user was unable to provide a ZIP code or city for the service address, and the agent attempted multiple times to obtain this information to check service coverage. The call ended abruptly with the user hanging up before providing the necessary details.

The user, Sarah Luckman, called to schedule a plumbing service for a leaking gasket under her sink at 3052 Cherokee Lane, Saint Charles, MO 63301. She provided her contact details and confirmed availability anytime for the appointment. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that a representative will reach out on the next business day to confirm the appointment.

Denise called to report an emergency issue with her air conditioning unit that only turns on intermittently, similar to a previous problem. The agent documented the issue, confirmed the service address, and escalated the case for follow-up on the next business day since it was after hours. Denise declined further assistance and ended the call.

Michael called to request a revised estimate for repairing a coil that was previously fixed but only worked for about two weeks. He explained that the technician had noted the coil was freezing, and Michael is preparing to sell his house and wants the estimate lowered from $1400. The call ended with the agent transferring Michael to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call successfully to another agent.
The user called and requested to speak with a real person instead of the virtual assistant. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent.
The user called and requested to speak with a live agent. The initial agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to another agent to assist the user further.

The user called to get more details about a water heater appointment for their dad, Peter Alkanani. The agent attempted to gather information but the user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was transferred successfully.
The user requested to speak with a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly without any meaningful conversation or task completion. The agent attempted to engage, but the user hung up quickly.
The user, Denise, returned a call to the company and the agent, Sophie, promptly transferred her to a live agent for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.

The user called Budget after receiving a call from them and wanted to know what it was about. The agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent for further assistance and successfully transferred the call.
The user called Budget and asked to speak with Amy. The agent informed the user that Amy was unavailable and then transferred the call to a live representative as requested.
The user called to request a refund for a service fee. The agent apologized for not having direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

The user, Vanessa, acknowledged a previous call from Home Shield and was promptly transferred by the agent, Sophie, to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 21 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called back after missing a previous call from the company. The agent acknowledged the callback and promptly transferred the user to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to inquire about the cost to fix their heating system after a service visit in February. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The agent initiated the call and requested the user's service address ZIP code, but the user hung up shortly after without responding.

The user called to request a dispatch for an HVAC upgrade and wanted the technician to be informed about the homeowner's interest in upgrading the AC unit. The agent gathered necessary details including the dispatch number and the user's role, then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to request drain line replacement service in Illinois, but the agent informed them that plumbing services are not offered in that area. The user declined further assistance and ended the call.
The user requested customer service and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Diwala McDowell, called to inquire about their scheduled appointment, but the agent was unable to find any appointment in the system. The user requested to be transferred to a live representative, and the call was successfully transferred.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with a specific lady they had talked to before. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.

The user called to schedule a service appointment related to their American Home Shield cooling system, which is not cooling properly. The agent gathered the necessary information and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The user was already waiting for a repair from the service department.
